disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden 2-hour onset of copious thick upper-airway secretions the child cannot clear, with rapidly worsening laboured breathing
trigger_question_examples: When did this start and how quickly did it change?
What is his breathing like right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on over about two hours and got much worse in the last hour — thick sticky mucus he can't clear and his breathing is fast and really laboured.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Central cyanosis — dark blue lips and perioral discolouration
trigger_question_examples: What colour are his lips and skin?
Have his lips or face changed colour?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His lips and around his mouth have gone dark blue.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Increased work of breathing — intercostal and tracheal-tug recession, nasal flaring, very high respiratory rate
trigger_question_examples: Is his chest or the base of his neck sucking in when he breathes?
Is he working hard to breathe?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — his chest and the notch at the bottom of his neck are pulling in with every breath and his nostrils are flaring, and he's breathing so fast we can't count it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Fever 38.6 C with a change in secretions — suggests an acute infective driver on top of the chronic condition
trigger_question_examples: Have the secretions changed in colour or amount?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's hot — 38.6 on the ear thermometer — and there's suddenly a lot more thick mucus than usual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reduced responsiveness / increasing lethargy beyond the child's baseline
trigger_question_examples: Is he more drowsy or less responsive than his normal baseline?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's much more floppy and drowsy than his usual self — he's barely responding to us now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Home oximeter reading 82% on room air with a heart rate around 165
trigger_question_examples: Do you have an oxygen monitor at home — what does it read?
What is his oxygen level and heart rate showing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The home monitor is showing 82% oxygen and a heart rate around 165.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known advanced lysosomal storage disease with kyphoscoliosis restricting lung expansion and recurrent aspiration-prone chest infections — the substrate that makes this deterioration high-risk
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any ongoing medical conditions?
Has anything like this happened before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has an inherited enzyme (lysosomal storage) condition that's been getting worse, a badly curved spine that makes his breathing shallow, and he gets frequent bad chest infections.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: We were told a long time ago what it's called — I can find the letters from the metabolic team if that helps.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: PRN sublingual hyoscine given an hour ago with no useful drying effect; continuous home suction failing to keep the airway clear
trigger_question_examples: Have you tried anything, and did it help?
Is the suction machine keeping up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We gave his under-the-tongue drying medicine about an hour ago and it hasn't helped, and the suction machine can't keep up with how thick it is.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Child receives palliative-oriented home care and has an advance care directive / complex care records kept at home; no seizure today despite intractable epilepsy
trigger_question_examples: Is there an advance care plan or directive for him?
Has he had any seizures today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's under palliative-oriented care and we have his advance care directive and medical records here. No seizure today.
